title: Methylation cell-type variance at TE boundaries is class-specific — LTR/LINE/DNA elements show ~0.019 cross-cell-type variance, SINE only 0.014, with TE-free regions at 0.018 as baseline

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'Methylation cell-type variance at TE boundaries is class-specific — LTR/LINE/DNA'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: Per-TE class assignment from RepeatMasker; autosomal TEs only.

BLUEPRINT/Roadmap WGBS methylation profiles for 6 hematopoietic cell types (monocyte, neutrophil, B_naive, T_naive, HSC, NK) at 500-bp bins flanking TE boundaries. Variance taken across the 6 cell types per bin.

operations.note: 21 500-bp offset bins across ±5 kb from each TE boundary (5' and 3').
Per-bin across-celltype methylation variance. 246,102 bins at LTR, 586,948 LINE, 58,493 DNA, 366,269 SINE, 218,087 TE-free (values in methylation_celltype_variance.csv).
Per-class mean of the per-bin cross-celltype variance.

statistics.name: mean_cross_celltype_methylation_variance_by_te_class
statistics.note: Mean per-bin variance across the 6 hematopoietic cell types, per TE class. Values from methylation_celltype_variance.csv. LTR highest (young, actively regulated), SINE lowest (ancient, constitutively methylated).

statistics.name: te_free_mean_cross_celltype_variance
statistics.note: TE-free baseline: a control bin population not flanking any TE. LTR > baseline > SINE.

statistics.name: mean_beta_overall_by_te_class
statistics.note: Mean methylation β per TE class. SINE is most methylated (0.78 — consistent with constitutive heavy methylation), TE-free least (0.71). The β and variance orderings are almost inverted: high β / low variance = constitutive, low β / high variance = regulated.

inference.justification: Four conjuncts license the class-specific regulation claim: (1) LTR variance > SINE variance — stat-vs-stat — the core dichotomy (young LTR regulated, ancient SINE constitutive); (2) LTR > TE-free baseline — stat-vs-stat — LTR regulation is real, not just inherited from genomic position; (3) SINE < TE-free baseline — stat-vs-stat — SINE is specifically constitutive, below background, the asymmetric complement to LTR; (4) absolute LTR variance > 0.015 ensures the signal is substantive rather than a marginal ranking. The three stat-vs-stat comparisons together establish LTR > baseline > SINE, the full class-specificity claim.
inference.failure_mode: If LTR variance fell to baseline, the 'young LTRs are the actively regulated fraction' framing collapses. If SINE variance rose above baseline, the constitutive-methylation claim for ancient elements fails. If the LTR > SINE direction reversed, the age-inverted regulation pattern would dissolve.

conclusion.assertion: Methylation cell-type variance at TE boundaries is class-specific: LTR elements show the highest cross-cell-type variance (0.0199 across 6 hematopoietic cell types), followed by LINE (0.0194) and DNA (0.0191); TE-free regions sit at 0.0178; SINE elements are lowest at 0.0140. The LTR > TE-free > SINE ordering — combined with inverse β-value rank (SINE highest mean β = 0.78, LTR lowest = 0.72) — supports the model that young LTR/ERV elements are the actively regulated fraction, while ancient SINEs are constitutively heavily methylated.

scope.scale_note: Boundary-flank scope (±5 kb). TE-interior methylation is a related but separate claim. Reference-only methylation (germline) — PCAWG somatic methylation dynamics are deferred per PAPER_OUTLINE § limitations.

confidence.note: Variance computed over n >= 58k bins per class; formal inferential test (e.g. per-class permutation null) staged but not pinned.

external_assumptions.statement: 6 BLUEPRINT hematopoietic cell types are sufficient to estimate cross-cell-type methylation variance at TE boundaries; additional tissues would increase variance estimates but preserve relative ordering.
1 kb effective resolution (500-bp bins) is an accepted lower bound for WGBS-based boundary analysis; finer resolution requires targeted bisulfite sequencing and is deferred.

TE-free regions with matched distance-to-TSS and GC distribution are a valid baseline; a more aggressive control (matched by gene-regulatory context) would further narrow the interpretation but would not change the LTR > baseline > SINE ordering.
